title: Value of Parents

pages.text: A mother bear, a father bear, and their baby bear lived happily together. The baby bear loved nothing more than playing all day long.
Whenever his parents told him it was time to learn and do some chores, he refused and ran off to play instead.
To help him learn, his parents began to punish him gently in order to correct his bad habits.
This made the baby bear very angry, so he ran away from home and crossed a deep river to hide from his parents.
At first, he played happily on the other side, but when he grew hungry, he realized there was no food anywhere.
Having no choice, he went to a clever fox's house to beg for something to eat.
The fox said, "If you do my chores, I will give you food," and then made the poor baby bear do all of his heavy work.
Working hard all day, the tired baby bear finally realized how wonderful his home and parents truly were.
Soon, the bear parents heard the sad news that their baby was working as a servant, and they rushed over to rescue him.
The baby bear cried, "Please forgive me, Mama and Papa! Now I finally understand the value of my parents and your wise words."
"From now on, I will always listen to you and obey you," the baby bear whispered, hugging his parents tightly with tears in his eyes.

moral: Parents always want what is best for us. Love, respect, and listen to their guidance because their wisdom helps us grow.
